hero.summary: Front-End React Developer with 9 years of experience, including 5 years fully remote in agile teams. I've built applications used by thousands of users, treating WCAG accessibility as a requirement, not an afterthought. Autonomous, proactive and comfortable working solo or in a team — I love turning complex needs into simple, solid and inclusive interfaces.

maskott.desc1: Built from scratch a web/mobile application (React + Capacitor) allowing learners to follow, review and be assessed on their Tactileo modules
maskott.desc2: Evolution and bug fixes on the pedagogical content Player — progressively took full ownership after an initial phase in a 3-developer front-end team
maskott.desc3: Migrated the existing React JS Design System to TypeScript
maskott.desc4: Full stack development of a subscription management tool via the GAR API (National Education Resource Access Manager) and usage tracking dashboard — mentored an intern
maskott.desc5: Contributions to the resource recommendation engine (Orchard project): new features and adjustments
maskott.desc6: WCAG accessibility compliance implementation on the learner application

ktm.desc1: Development and evolution of an Angular component library for e-learning module creation (JSON structure + reusable components)
ktm.desc2: Design and delivery of 15+ training modules for major clients (Bouygues TP, TotalEnergies, PSA, Agreenium...)
ktm.desc3: Full redesign of the Back-Office dedicated to GRDF agent retraining
ktm.desc4: Autonomous work across multiple simultaneous Angular projects (JS and TS)

hippocad.desc1: Built from scratch a mobile-first web application (ExtJS) for carer/family liaison — replacing the paper patient follow-up booklet
hippocad.desc2: Developed all front-end features: authentication, patient records, liaison messaging, responsive multi-profile UI (carers, home helpers, family)
hippocad.desc3: Maintenance and evolution of an existing Android/Java application
hippocad.desc4: Sole front-end developer, working in tandem with the back-end developer

maskott.desc: Interactive module player (MCQ, drag & drop, fill-in-the-blank, video, educational content...), resource recommendation engine, Design System, WCAG 2.1 Accessibility.
maskott.longDesc: Interactive module player (MCQ, drag & drop, fill-in-the-blank, video, educational content...), resource recommendation engine, full Design System, WCAG 2.1 accessibility. Agile team, code reviews, continuous integration.

portfolio.desc: Portfolio built like a real product: dark/light mode, accessibility menu, fr/en i18n, real-time test dashboard and secure contact API.
portfolio.longDesc: Portfolio built like a real product: dark/light mode, accessibility menu, fr/en i18n, real-time test dashboard, WebP optimization. Monorepo architecture with independent front-end and API.

weather.desc: Connected weather station on Raspberry Pi — real-time temperature, humidity and pressure readings.
weather.longDesc: IoT project currently in development. The station will be connected to the portfolio to display live weather data.

brainboost.desc: Flashcard app with spaced repetition (SM-2 algorithm), AI-powered card generation from handwritten notes, multiple study modes and push notifications.
brainboost.longDesc: SM-2 algorithm implemented from scratch, 5 study modes (flashcards, MCQ, written test, match, drag-drop). AI-powered card generation from handwritten notes, VAPID push notifications, seasonal themes. 233 tests (89.6% coverage) + 20 Playwright E2E with WCAG AA validation.

playground.title: Playground
playground.subtitle: Accessible React components, tested and documented. Keyboard navigation, ARIA, focus management — best practices in action.

modal.title: Accessible Modal
modal.desc: Focus trap, scroll lock, focus return, Escape to close.
modal.demoDesc: This modal implements all accessibility best practices:

features.focusReturn: Focus returns to trigger on close

featureList.focusTrap: Focus trap — focus stays in the modal

toast.title: Toast Notifications
toast.desc: Queue system, aria-live, auto and manual close.

accordion.title: Accessible Accordion
accordion.desc: Full keyboard navigation, aria-expanded, single or multi open.

a11y.title: ♿ Why web accessibility?
a11y.content: 15% of the world's population lives with a disability. An accessible interface benefits everyone — screen reader users, keyboard navigation, elderly people, low connectivity contexts. It's a right, not an option.

aria.title: 🏷 ARIA — when and how to use it?
aria.content: ARIA (Accessible Rich Internet Applications) enhances semantic HTML. Golden rule: don't use ARIA if native HTML suffices. A <button> is always preferable to a <div role="button">.
aria.rule: don't use ARIA if native HTML suffices

focus.title: ⌨️ Keyboard focus management
focus.content: Every interactive element must be reachable by keyboard (Tab) and have a visible focus indicator. Modals require a focus trap — focus must not escape to background content.

contrast.title: 🎨 Contrast and colors
contrast.content: WCAG 2.1 level AA requires a contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text. Never convey information through color alone.
